zh.lifeSummary: 當William Leonard於1510年出生在英國肯特澤文亞可斯時，他的父親Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II31歲，母親Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston29歲。他於1560年1月13日與Ann Perkins在英國肯特艾登布利其結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1588年7月30日在英國肯特設維寧享年78歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Leonard于1510年出生在英国肯特澤文亞可斯时，他的父亲Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II31岁，母亲Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston29岁。他于1560年1月13日与Ann Perkins在英国肯特艾登布利其结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1588年7月30日在英国肯特設維寧享年78岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Leonard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English; French (Léonard); Walloon (mainly Léonard): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements leo ‘lion’ (a late addition to the vocabulary of ancient Germanic name elements, taken from Latin) + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’, which was taken to England by the Normans. A Christian saint of this name, who is supposed to have lived in the 6th century, but about whom nothing is known except for a largely fictional life dating from half a millennium later, was popular throughout Europe in the early Middle Ages and was regarded as the patron of peasants and horses. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Leonardo , Polish, Slovenian, etc. Lenart or Lenard , and probably also their derivatives. Compare Larned , Learned , and Yenor .
Irish (Fermanagh): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Fhionáin or of Langan .
German: variant of   Leonhard  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Leonardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Fernando, Carlos, Ernesto, Felipe, Guillermo, Julio, Luz, Mario, Alberto, Altagracia.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Carmelo, Dario, Ercole, Filomena, Giuseppe, Marco, Mauro, Oreste, Pasco, Quintino.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements leo ‘lion’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’ (see Leonard ).

variants.name: Langan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Declan.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Longáin ‘descendant of Longán’, a personal name probably derived from long ‘tall’, or perhaps from a homonymous long ‘ship’ (and so originally a byname for a seafarer). Sometimes Leonard is used as an equivalent.

variants.name: Learnard
meanings: Altered form of English   Leonard  . Compare   Learned  .

variants.name: Lenhard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hermann, Kurt, Urs.
Dutch and North German: from the personal name Leonhard (see Leonard ).

variants.name: Lenhart
meanings: North German, and Czech: from the personal name Leonhard (see Leonard , Lenart ).

variants.name: Lence
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Joaquin, Caridad, Carmella, Delfina, Domingo, Fidelia, Marta, Roberto, Sergio.
Galician: habitational name from any of the four places called Lence.
Slovenian (Lenče): from a pet form of the personal names Lenart (see Leonard ) and Magdalena (see Maudlin ).

variants.name: Lehn
meanings: German:
from a short form of the personal name Leonhard, see Leonard .
habitational name from any of various places of that name.

variants.name: Learned
meanings: Altered form of English   Leonard  . An intermediate stage in this change is   Learnard  . Compare   Larned  .

History: William Learned was admitted to the First Church in Charlestown, MA, in 1632, and in 1640 was one of the first settlers of Woburn, MA. One of his better known descendants, Ebenezer, a revolutionary war officer, was born at Oxford, MA, in 1728.

variants.name: Lenard
meanings: English and Irish; French (Lénard): variant of Leonard , French Léonard.

Polish, German, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Lénárd); Slovak (also Lenárd and Lénárd): from local vernacular forms of the personal name Leonard (German Leonhard ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of patronymics (and other derivatives) from this name, such as Croatian Lenardić and Slovenian Lenardič.

variants.name: Lenart
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Agnieszka, Bronislawa, Henryk, Henryka, Jacek, Janina, Leslaw, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Witold, Zofia.

Polish, Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, and German; Slovak (Lenárt); Hungarian (Lénárt): from the personal name Lenart, Slovak Lenárt, Hungarian Lénárt, equivalents of Leonard (German Leonhard ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of patronymics (and other derivatives) from this name, such as Polish Lenartowicz , Slovenian Lenarčič and Lenartič, Croatian Lenartić.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When William Leonard was born in 1510, in Sevenoaks, Kent, England, his father, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, was 31 and his mother, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, was 29. He married Ann Perkins on 13 January 1560, in Edenbridge, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 30 July 1588, in Chevening, Kent, England, at the age of 78, and was buried in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Leonard est né en 1510, à Sevenoaks, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Ann Perkins le 13 janvier 1560, à Edenbridge, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 30 juillet 1588, à Chevening, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Leonard 1510 in Sevenoaks, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II 31 und seine Mutter Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston 29. Er hat am 13. Januar 1560 in Edenbridge, Kent, England Ann Perkins geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 30. Juli 1588 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Chevening, Kent, England und wurde in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Leonard è nato il nel 1510 nel Sevenoaks, Kent, England, il suo padre, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Ann Perkins il 13 gennaio 1560, nel Edenbridge, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 30 luglio 1588, nel Chevening, Kent, England, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.Saint Botolph's Church, Chevening, Seven: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: William Leonard(이)가 1510년 영국 켄트 세베노크스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1560년 1월 13일 영국 켄트 에덴브리지에서 Ann Perkins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1588년 7월 30일 영국 켄트 처베닝에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Leonard nasceu em 1510, em Sevenoaks, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Ann Perkins em 13 de janeiro de 1560, em Edenbridge, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 30 de julho de 1588, em Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Leonard родился в 1510 г. в местности сивеноакс, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Perkins 13 января 1560 г. в местности эденбридж, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 30 июля 1588 г. в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Ann Perkins el 13 de enero de 1560, en Edenbridge, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija.
